name: Color Contrast
dos: Maintain minimum 4.5:1 ratio for normal text, 3:1 for large text (18px+ bold or 24px+ regular). Test all color pairs with contrast checkers.
donts: Use low-contrast text on backgrounds, rely on color alone to convey information, use gray-on-gray text.
keywords: contrast accessibility color ratio WCAG text readability

name: Focus States
dos: Provide visible focus indicators (2-4px ring, 2px offset) on all interactive elements. Ensure focus order matches visual layout.
donts: Remove outline: none without providing custom focus styles. Hide focus indicators from keyboard users.
keywords: focus keyboard accessibility outline ring navigation tab

name: Alt Text
dos: Provide descriptive alt text for meaningful images. Decorative images get alt="". Icons in buttons need aria-label.
donts: Leave alt text empty for informational images. Use file names as alt text ("image001.jpg"). Overdescribe obvious content.
keywords: alt text accessibility image screen-reader aria description

name: Keyboard Navigation
dos: Ensure all functionality is available via keyboard (Tab, Enter, Escape, Arrow keys). Tab order must match visual order.
donts: Trap keyboard focus in modals without escape. Use tabindex > 0. Require mouse-only interactions.
keywords: keyboard navigation tab focus accessibility aria screen-reader

name: Form Labels
dos: Every form field needs a visible label with proper for/htmlFor attribute. Labels must describe field purpose.
donts: Use placeholder text as the only label. Hide labels visually. Use generic labels like 'Field 1'.
keywords: form label accessibility input field placeholder aria

name: Heading Hierarchy
dos: Use sequential heading levels (h1 → h2 → h3). One h1 per page. Headings describe the content that follows.
donts: Skip heading levels (h1 → h3). Use headings for visual styling instead of structure. Have multiple h1 elements.
keywords: heading hierarchy h1 h2 h3 structure accessibility semantic

name: Reduced Motion
dos: Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query. Reduce or disable animations, parallax, and auto-playing content.
donts: Ignore user motion preferences. Use large-scale animations without reduced-motion alternatives.
keywords: motion animation accessibility reduced-motion prefers-reduced-motion vestibular

name: Screen Reader Support
dos: Use semantic HTML, ARIA landmarks, and proper roles. Test with VoiceOver/NVDA. Announce dynamic content changes.
donts: Use div for interactive elements. Forget aria-live for dynamic updates. Use aria roles incorrectly.
keywords: screen-reader aria voiceover nvda semantic accessibility

name: Touch Target Size
dos: Minimum 44×44pt (iOS) / 48×48dp (Android) touch targets. Expand hit area with padding or hitSlop.
donts: Use small tap targets without extra padding. Place interactive elements too close together (< 8px gap).
keywords: touch target size mobile iOS Android accessibility hit area

name: Touch Spacing
dos: Minimum 8px/8dp gap between touch targets to prevent mis-taps. Group related controls with adequate spacing.
donts: Cram buttons together. Place links too close in text. Overlap interactive regions.
keywords: touch spacing gap mobile mis-tap interaction padding

name: Hover vs Tap
dos: Primary interactions must work with click/tap. Hover is enhancement, not requirement. Show tap feedback.
donts: Rely on hover alone for critical actions. Use hover-only tooltips on mobile.
keywords: hover tap touch mobile interaction click feedback

name: Loading Feedback
dos: Show loading state within 100ms of action. Use skeleton screens for content > 300ms. Disable buttons during async operations.
donts: Leave users waiting without feedback. Use generic spinners for content loading. Enable double-submit.
keywords: loading feedback skeleton spinner async button disabled submit

name: Image Optimization
dos: Use WebP/AVIF formats, responsive images with srcset/sizes, lazy load non-critical images, declare dimensions.
donts: Serve unoptimized PNGs, skip responsive images, forget to set width/height (causes CLS).
keywords: image optimization webp avif lazy-load responsive srcset performance

name: Font Loading
dos: Use font-display: swap/optional, preload critical fonts, subset fonts to needed characters.
donts: Use font-display: block (causes FOIT), load all font variants, use web fonts for small text.
keywords: font loading performance FOIT font-display swap preload

name: Layout Shift Prevention
dos: Reserve space for images (width/height), ads, embeds, and dynamic content. Use aspect-ratio CSS property.
donts: Let images and embeds push content down after load. Use 100vh without min-h-dvh fallback.
keywords: CLS layout-shift performance cumulative aspect-ratio image

name: Lazy Loading
dos: Lazy load below-the-fold content, images, and route components. Use Intersection Observer or loading="lazy".
donts: Lazy load above-the-fold content. Eagerly load all images on initial page load.
keywords: lazy loading intersection-observer below-fold performance

name: Virtual Lists
dos: Virtualize lists with 50+ items. Use react-window, @tanstack/virtual, or FlashList for React Native.
donts: Render all items in a long list at once. Measure performance only with small datasets.
keywords: virtual list performance long-list react-window flashlist

name: Style Consistency
dos: Use the same style consistently across all pages and components. Define design tokens for colors, spacing, shadows.
donts: Mix different styles (glassmorphism + flat) on the same page. Use inconsistent shadow depths.
keywords: style consistency design-tokens theme visual-cohesion

name: No Emoji Icons
dos: Use vector SVG icons from libraries like Heroicons, Lucide, or Phosphor. Consistent stroke width and style.
donts: Use emojis as UI icons (navigation, actions, settings). Emojis render differently across platforms.
keywords: icons svg vector emoji heroicons lucide phosphor

name: Mobile-First Design
dos: Design for the smallest screen first (375px), then scale up. Use mobile-first media queries (min-width).
donts: Design desktop-first and squeeze down. Use max-width media queries for mobile.
keywords: mobile-first responsive design 375px min-width progressive-enhancement

name: Readable Font Size
dos: Minimum 16px body text on mobile to prevent iOS auto-zoom. Use rem/em units, not px for text.
donts: Use font-size below 16px on mobile. Set font-size in px only. Ignore system font-size settings.
keywords: font-size 16px mobile readable rem responsive text

name: Horizontal Scroll Prevention
dos: Ensure all content fits within viewport width. Use overflow-x: hidden on body. Test on 375px screens.
donts: Use fixed-width containers. Let long words or code blocks overflow. Use tables without horizontal scroll handling.
keywords: horizontal-scroll responsive overflow mobile layout break

name: Spacing Scale
dos: Use consistent 4pt/8px incremental spacing system. Define spacing tokens (xs: 4, sm: 8, md: 16, lg: 24, xl: 32, 2xl: 48).
donts: Use arbitrary spacing values. Mix px and rem without consistent scale. Vary spacing by developer preference.
keywords: spacing scale 8px grid consistency tokens layout rhythm

name: Font Pairing
dos: Choose complementary typefaces: one for headings (expressive) and one for body (readable). Limit to 2 fonts.
donts: Use more than 2 font families. Pair two expressive fonts. Use similar fonts with minimal contrast.
keywords: typography font-pairing headings body typeface contrast harmony

name: Semantic Color Tokens
dos: Define semantic colors (primary, secondary, success, error, warning, info, surface, on-surface). Use CSS custom properties.
donts: Hardcode hex values in components. Name colors by their value (blue, red) instead of purpose (primary, danger).
keywords: semantic color tokens variables CSS custom-properties theme

name: Animation Duration
dos: Use 150-300ms for micro-interactions, 300-400ms for complex transitions. Faster for exit (60-70% of enter).
donts: Use animations >500ms for UI elements. Use linear easing. Use same duration for all animations.
keywords: animation duration timing easing transitions 300ms micro-interaction

name: Transform Performance
dos: Use transform and opacity for animations. These trigger compositing only, not layout/paint.
donts: Animate width, height, top, left, margin, or padding (trigger layout).
keywords: transform opacity animation performance compositing layout paint

name: Motion Meaning
dos: Every animation should express cause-effect (what happened, where it came from). Motion provides spatial continuity.
donts: Use decorative-only animation. Animate without purpose. Add motion just for visual flair.
keywords: motion meaning animation purpose spatial-continuity transition

name: Input Labels
dos: Every input needs a visible label above or beside the field. Labels describe what to enter and any format requirements.
donts: Use placeholder as the only label. Stack labels inside inputs (disappears on typing). Use generic labels.
keywords: form label input field placeholder accessibility visible

name: Error Placement
dos: Show error messages below the related field, not at top of form. Use color + icon + text for errors.
donts: Show all errors only at the top of the form. Use color alone to indicate errors. Hide error messages.
keywords: error placement form validation feedback inline message

name: Submit Feedback
dos: Show loading state during submission, then success confirmation or error. Disable submit button after click.
donts: Let double-submit happen. Leave form unchanged after error. Navigate away without saving draft.
keywords: submit feedback loading success error button async

name: Bottom Navigation
dos: Bottom navigation: max 5 items, use icon + label, highlight active state, preserve scroll on tab switch.
donts: Use more than 5 bottom nav items, use icon-only bottom nav, reset scroll on tab switch unexpectedly.
keywords: bottom-navigation tab navigation mobile icon label

name: Back Navigation
dos: Back navigation must be predictable: go to previous screen, preserve scroll position and input state.
donts: Go to unexpected screens on back. Reset form state on back. Use back button where user doesn't expect it.
keywords: back navigation predict stack history scroll preserve

name: Deep Linking
dos: All key screens must be reachable via deep link. Support universal links / app links for cross-app navigation.
donts: Force users to start from home screen. Break deep links after app updates.
keywords: deep-link universal-link app-link navigation screen routing

name: Chart Type Selection
dos: Match chart type to data: trend → line, comparison → bar, composition → pie/donut (max 5 categories), distribution → histogram.
donts: Use pie charts for >5 categories. Use 3D charts (distorts perception). Use area charts for non-cumulative data.
keywords: chart type selection line bar pie histogram data visualization

name: Accessible Color for Charts
dos: Use accessible color palettes designed for colorblind viewers. Supplement with patterns and textures.
donts: Use red/green pairs alone for differentiating data. Rely on color as the only differentiator.
keywords: chart color accessible colorblind palette pattern texture

name: Chart Tooltips
dos: Show tooltips on hover/tap with exact values. Tooltips must be keyboard accessible. Position to avoid overflow.
donts: Hide tooltips behind chart boundaries. Require hover-only for data exploration. Show no data labels.
keywords: chart tooltip hover tap values data label accessible

name: Interaction States
dos: Every interactive element needs: normal, hover, focus, active/pressed, disabled states. Visually distinct transitions.
donts: Skip hover states. Use same style for active and focus. Keep disabled elements without visual change.
keywords: interaction states hover focus active disabled transitions CSS

name: Dark Mode Parity
dos: Design light and dark variants together. Test contrast separately in each mode. Use semantic tokens that map per theme.
donts: Invert colors mechanically. Design light mode first, dark as afterthought. Skip dark mode testing.
keywords: dark-mode light-mode theme contrast parity tokens

name: Empty States
dos: Show meaningful empty state: illustration, message explaining why empty, and action CTA. No blank screens.
donts: Show blank screen or loading spinner forever. Show developer-facing error messages to users.
keywords: empty-state no-data illustration message CTA onboarding

name: Confirmation Dialogs
dos: Confirm before destructive actions (delete, remove, cancel with unsaved changes). Show what will be lost.
donts: Skip confirmation for irreversible actions. Use unclear confirmation text ("Are you sure?").
keywords: confirmation dialog destructive delete undo warning

name: Safe Areas
dos: Respect device safe areas (notch, Dynamic Island, gesture bar). Use env(safe-area-inset-*) for web.
donts: Place critical UI behind notches or under gesture bars. Ignore device-specific insets.
keywords: safe-area notch dynamic-island gesture-bar inset mobile

name: Progressive Disclosure
dos: Show advanced/complex options progressively. Start simple, reveal complexity on demand.
donts: Overwhelm users with all options upfront. Hide essential controls in menus.
keywords: progressive disclosure simple complex reveal advanced options

name: Touch Feedback Speed
dos: Provide visual feedback within 100ms of tap (ripple, highlight, opacity). Users perceive <100ms as instant.
donts: Delay visual feedback until async operation completes. Use no touch feedback at all.
keywords: touch feedback 100ms ripple highlight instant response

name: Gesture Alternatives
dos: Always provide visible controls for critical actions. Gestures are shortcuts, not the only path.
donts: Make swipe-to-delete the only way to delete. Use multi-finger gestures for primary actions.
keywords: gesture alternative visible control swipe delete accessibility

name: System Navigation Respect
dos: Don't block system gestures (back swipe, Control Center, notification center). Keep interactive zones clear.
donts: Override system back gesture. Place interactive elements near screen edges where system gestures live.
keywords: system gesture swipe-back control-center navigation mobile

name: URL Structure
dos: Use clear, readable URLs that reflect app structure. Lowercase with hyphens. Support direct access.
donts: Use nested query parameters. Use IDs only without readable slugs. Break URLs on restructure without redirects.
keywords: url structure slug routing readable redirect seo

name: Pull-to-Refresh
dos: Support pull-to-refresh on scrollable content. Show clear loading indicator. Preserve scroll position after refresh.
donts: Push-to-refresh on non-scrollable content. Reset scroll position to top after refresh.
keywords: pull-to-refresh gesture loading indicator scroll mobile

name: Skeleton Screens
dos: Use skeleton screens / shimmer placeholders for content loading >300ms. Match skeleton shape to content layout.
donts: Use generic spinners for content. Show blank boxes. Use animated skeletons that don't match content shape.
keywords: skeleton screen shimmer placeholder loading content layout

name: Optimistic Updates
dos: Show immediate UI feedback for expected-success actions. Revert on failure with clear error.
donts: Show loading for every action. Leave stale data visible after user action.
keywords: optimistic update immediate feedback success revert error

name: Search Experience
dos: Show search prominently (top bar or accessible icon). Provide recent searches, autocomplete, clear button.
donts: Hide search in menus. Require exact match. Clear search on back navigation.
keywords: search autocomplete recent clear navigation filter

name: Onboarding Flow
dos: Show essential features only in onboarding. Skip-able, re-playable, contextual (not all at once).
donts: Force through 10-step onboarding before using app. Show all features at once. No skip option.
keywords: onboarding tutorial contextual skip welcome first-use

name: Micro-copy Quality
dos: Use clear, concise, human language. Explain errors in plain English. CTAs describe action ("Save changes" not "Submit").
donts: Use technical jargon. Use passive voice in error messages. Use vague CTAs ("Click here", "Submit").
keywords: micro-copy text copywriting CTA error message clear concise

name: Platform Conventions
dos: Follow platform HIG (iOS Human Interface Guidelines / Material Design). Use platform-standard components and patterns.
donts: Use iOS patterns on Android and vice versa. Create custom components that behave differently from platform standards.
keywords: platform HIG iOS material-design android convention standard

name: Responsive Breakpoints
dos: Test at 375px (small phone), 768px (tablet portrait), 1024px (tablet landscape/desktop), 1440px (desktop).
donts: Test only on desktop. Use device-specific breakpoints. Forget tablet layouts.
keywords: responsive breakpoints 375 768 1024 1440 mobile tablet desktop

name: Consistent Shadow Elevation
dos: Define shadow elevation scale (0-5). Cards get elevation 1, modals get elevation 3, tooltips get elevation 5.
donts: Use random shadow values. Same elevation for all elements. Skip shadows entirely on flat design.
keywords: shadow elevation card modal tooltip depth consistency

name: Icon Stroke Consistency
dos: Use consistent stroke width within same hierarchy level. Typically 1.5-2px for UI icons.
donts: Mix stroke widths arbitrarily. Use filled and outline at same level.
keywords: icon stroke width consistency outline filled hierarchy

name: Cursor Indicators
dos: cursor: pointer on all clickable elements. cursor: not-allowed on disabled. cursor: default on text.
donts: Forget cursor styles on interactive elements. Use pointer on non-interactive elements.
keywords: cursor pointer clickable not-allowed disabled interactive

name: Readable Line Length
dos: Limit line length to 60-75 characters for readability. Use max-width on text containers.
donts: Full-width text across large screens. Overly narrow text columns that require constant scrolling.
keywords: line-length readability typography max-width characters screen

name: Z-Index Management
dos: Define layered z-index scale: dropdowns 100, sticky 200, nav 300, modals 400, toasts 500, tooltips 600.
donts: Use random z-index values. z-index: 9999 everywhere. Stacking context conflicts.
keywords: z-index stacking context dropdown modal toast tooltip layer

name: Error Recovery
dos: Every error message must include: what happened, why it happened, and how to fix it. Provide retry action.
donts: Show "Something went wrong" without details. No retry option. Dismiss error permanently.
keywords: error recovery retry message fix action help

name: Network State Handling
dos: Handle offline, slow network (3G), and server error states. Show appropriate UI for each: offline banner, skeleton for slow, error + retry for server.
donts: Show loading spinner forever on network failure. Crash on offline. No offline caching.
keywords: offline network error 3G slow loading fallback cache

name: State Management Clarity
dos: Each screen has clear states: loading (skeleton), empty (CTAs), error (message + action), success (data).
donts: Mix error and loading states. Show empty data without explanation. Skip loading state for fast operations.
keywords: state loading empty error success data flow screen

name: Form Autofill
dos: Use proper autocomplete attributes for forms (name, email, address, credit card). Enable browser password managers.
donts: Disable autofill with autocomplete="off". Use non-standard field names that confuse password managers.
keywords: autofill form browser password-manager autocomplete attribute

name: Toast Notification
dos: Auto-dismiss notifications in 3-5 seconds. Non-blocking, don't steal focus. Use aria-live="polite" for screen readers.
donts: Require manual dismiss for all toasts. Stack toasts infinitely. Use toasts for critical errors (use modal/alert).
keywords: toast notification dismiss timer aria-live non-blocking

name: Password Input Design
dos: Provide show/hide toggle. Show password strength indicator. Allow paste into password fields.
donts: Mask password with no show option. Block paste. Use restrictive character rules without explanation.
keywords: password input show hide toggle strength paste

name: Multi-step Forms
dos: Show progress indicator. Allow back navigation. Save draft on each step. Show step count (Step 2 of 5).
donts: No progress indicator. Disable back navigation. Lose data on accidental close.
keywords: multi-step form progress wizard step indicator draft save

name: Button Hierarchy
dos: Primary (filled), secondary (outlined), tertiary (text) button hierarchy. One primary CTA per view.
donts: Multiple primary buttons competing for attention. No visual distinction between button levels.
keywords: button hierarchy primary secondary tertiary CTA filled outlined

name: Loading States for Images
dos: Show blur-up placeholder, low-res preview, or skeleton while image loads. Use aspect-ratio to reserve space.
donts: Show broken image icon. Let images pop in without space reserved. No loading state.
keywords: image loading blur placeholder skeleton aspect-ratio progressive

name: Input Type & Keyboard
dos: Use semantic input types (email, tel, number, url, search) to trigger correct mobile keyboard.
donts: Use type="text" for all inputs. Miss inputmode for numeric fields.
keywords: input type keyboard mobile email tel number keyboard

name: Accessible Forms
dos: Associate labels with inputs via for/id, group related fields with fieldset/legend, show error summaries at top.
donts: Orphaned inputs without labels. No grouping for related fields. Invisible error messages.
keywords: accessible form fieldset legend label error summary WCAG

name: Text Scaling Support
dos: Support system font scaling (Dynamic Type on iOS, font size on Android). Use relative units (rem/em). Test at largest setting.
donts: Lock font sizes with px. Clip or truncate text at large sizes. Break layout at max font size.
keywords: text scaling dynamic-type font-size accessibility rem em large

name: Touch Deferral
dos: Use touch-action: manipulation on interactive elements to eliminate 300ms tap delay on mobile browsers.
donts: Ignore tap delay on mobile web. Use click events only without touch event optimization.
keywords: tap-delay touch-action manipulation mobile web 300ms

name: Content Priority
dos: Show most important content first on mobile. Fold or hide secondary content behind 'Show more' or navigation.
donts: Show all content equally prioritized. Bury critical actions below the fold.
keywords: content priority mobile fold above-the-fold show-more

name: Nested Scroll
dos: Avoid nested scrollable regions that interfere with main scroll. If needed, use overflow: auto with clear indicator.
donts: Nested scroll containers without fixed dimensions. Scroll-jacking the main scroll with inner scrolls.
keywords: nested scroll overflow scroll-container interference mobile

name: Orientation Support
dos: Keep layout readable and operable in both portrait and landscape. Don't lock orientation unless critical.
donts: Break layout in landscape mode. Force portrait only without reason. Hide content in landscape.
keywords: orientation portrait landscape rotation responsive mobile

name: Accessible Colors
dos: Use accessible color combinations. Test with colorblind simulators (deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia). Don't use color alone.
donts: Red/green as only differentiator. Low contrast for subtle UI elements. Use only color to show state.
keywords: colorblind accessible color deuteranopia protanopia contrast

name: Video & Audio Accessibility
dos: Provide captions for video, transcripts for audio. Don't autoplay. Provide play/pause controls.
donts: Autoplay video with sound. No captions. No pause control.
keywords: video audio captions transcripts autoplay accessibility controls

name: Skip Navigation Link
dos: Provide a 'Skip to main content' link as the first focusable element. Helps keyboard and screen reader users.
donts: Skip navigation for keyboard users. Hide skip link visually without making it available on focus.
keywords: skip-navigation keyboard accessibility screen-reader skip-link

name: Data Export
dos: Offer data export (CSV, PDF, image) for charts, tables, and reports. Follow platform share sheet patterns.
donts: Lock data inside UI without export. Use obscure export formats. No share option.
keywords: export csv pdf image chart data share download

name: Undo Support
dos: Support undo for destructive and bulk actions. Show 'Undo' toast/button after action with 5-10s window.
donts: No undo for destroy actions. Immediate permanent changes without confirmation.
keywords: undo toast action destructive bulk recovery confirmation

name: Passwordless Auth
dos: Support magic links, OTP codes, and biometric auth (Face ID / fingerprint) as alternatives to passwords.
donts: Force password-only auth. Require complex passwords without alternative. Send OTP that takes long to arrive.
keywords: passwordless magic-link otp biometric face-id auth

name: Pull-to-Refresh
dos: Pull-to-refresh on scrollable content with clear loading indicator. Haptic feedback on mobile when activated.
donts: Pull-to-refresh on non-updateable content. Conflict with other scroll gestures.
keywords: pull refresh gesture indicator loading haptic mobile

name: Share Sheet Integration
dos: Use platform share sheet (not custom). Include relevant content (link, text, image). Respect privacy.
donts: Build custom share UI. Share without user preview. Include tracking parameters without disclosure.
keywords: share sheet platform native content link privacy

name: Contextual Help
dos: Provide help text near complex controls. Tooltip icon (?) for explanations. FAQ within the flow, not separate page.
donts: Bury help in separate docs site. Assume users understand all controls. No inline guidance.
keywords: help contextual tooltip FAQ guidance inline support
